## Problem

Simplistic binary Merkle tree with keccak hash. Fixed depth of 10 (2^10 elements). Tree root is given in public input, Merkle path given in private.

## Merke trees primer

Binary Merkle tree of depth 3 with 8 leaves (a..h). `H(x)` is the hash of `x` and `||` for concatenation.

```
                            root
                       H(N4 || N5)
                      /            \
                    /                \
                  N4                  N5
            H(N0 || N1)         H(N2 || N3)
              /      \              /      \
            /          \          /          \
          N0            N1      N2            N3
      H(a || b)     H(c || d) H(e || f)  H(g || h)
        /   \         /   \     /   \       /   \
       a     b       c     d   e     f     g     h
```

**Merkle proof (membership path):** To prove that leaf `c` is in the tree you
only need to reveal 3 sibling hashes along the path from `c` to the root:
`d` (sibling of `c`), `N0` (sibling of `N1`), and `N5` (sibling of `N4`).
The verifier recomputes `N1 = H(c || d)`, then `N4 = H(N0 || N1)`, then
`root = H(N4 || N5)` and checks it matches the known root. This takes
`O(log n)` hashes instead of reading all `n` leaves.

## Sample values 

Sibling hashes are chosen to be keccak256("i") for i in 0..10, privateElement to be keccak256("10").

Public root computes to be `0x0137f741382e3d72449611e4398fe12dd9d46196b6b6a1545005a209c885617b`.
